local_results.title: Portland Museum of Art
local_results.description: Portland's premiere art museum. I.M. Pei-designed museum known for its paintings by Winslow Homer, Andrew Wyeth & Edward Hopper.

local_results.title: Children's Museum & Theatre of Maine
local_results.description: Hands-on learning for kids. Interactive kids' museum known for its camera obscura, also with science & job-related activities.

local_results.title: Maine Historical Society and the Wadsworth-Longfellow House
local_results.description: Celebrated poet's childhood home. 1800s home of poet Henry Wadsworth Longfellow, with estate furnishings, a garden & tours.

local_results.title: Victoria Mansion
local_results.description: Grand historical landmark with tours. This 19th-century Italian villa–style home with an ornate interior offers tours & a museum shop.

local_results.title: Fifth Maine Museum
local_results.description: Civil War-era exhibits & ocean views. 19th-century Memorial Hall with Civil War-era artifacts & photos, plus a veranda with ocean views.

local_results.title: Tate House Museum
local_results.description: Colonial-era home with historical tours. Circa-1755, clapboard home of a British naval officer, featuring period furnishings, with tours.

local_results.title: Umbrella Cover Museum
local_results.description: Whimsical locale with a big collection. Quirky museum with a colorful collection of over 1,300 umbrella covers from more than 60 countries.

local_results.title: Portland Observatory
local_results.description: Historic maritime signal tower & museum. Seasonal tours of the only remaining wooden maritime signal tower (built circa 1807) in the U.S.

local_results.title: Portland Art Museum
local_results.description: Asian, American & Native American art. Collection of Asian, American, Native American & European art, plus touring shows & film screenings.

local_results.title: International Cryptozoology Museum
local_results.description: Bigfoot-related items & more. Tiny museum with limited hours exploring the mystery of Bigfoot, Loch Ness Monster, mermaids & more.

local_results.title: University of New England Art Gallery
local_results.description: Campus gallery with rotating exhibits. Modern gallery featuring rotating exhibitions in various mediums, plus a sculpture garden.

local_results.title: Fort McKinley Museum at Diamond Cove
Etz Chaim Synagogue
local_results.description: Historic synagogue with a museum. Functioning European-style synagogue founded in 1921, with a museum displaying local Jewish artwork.

local_results.title: Neal Dow House
local_results.description: Historic 1800s home & landmark. Federal-style 1829 residence of local political leader & prohibitionist, with by-appointment tours.

local_results.title: Seashore Trolley Museum
local_results.description: Museum with public transit vehicles. Long-running indoor-outdoor museum featuring restored public transit vehicles & artifacts.

local_results.title: Farnsworth Art Museum
local_results.description: Works by prominent American artists. Paintings & sculptures by renowned American artists with Maine connections, most notably the Wyeths.
